name: Camino Francés: Villava to Zubiri
description: Cycle the easy 19.7-mile Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri route, enjoying rural landscapes, medieval bridges, and Pyrenean foothills views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villava/Atarrabia offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and mountain passes. The region provides varied terrain suitable for different levels of road cyclists, from gentle routes along the Camino Francés to more challenging climbs. Elevations vary, with some routes featuring significant ascents through areas like the Erro Pass and Belate Pass. Best road cycling routes around Villava/Atarrabia The most popular road cycling route is Erro Pass - Zubiri Loop from Pamplona, a 35.6 miles (57.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features a notable climb over the Erro Pass. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Camino Francés: Villava to Zubiri, an easy 19.7 miles (31.7 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Camino Francés, offering a relatively flat profile. Local road cyclists also love the Aranguren Valley Loop, a 33.3 miles (53.6 km) trail leading through the Aranguren Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Villava/Atarrabia is defined by river valleys, mountain passes, and reservoirs, with options for easy rides and more challenging climbs. The network offers routes for various ability levels. The routes in Villava/Atarrabia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 19,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villava/Atarrabia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Yes, Villava/Atarrabia offers several routes ideal for beginners. Approximately 12 easy routes are available, often featuring gentler profiles. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984940826/camino-frances-villava-to-zubiri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Villava to Zubiri&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a relatively flat section of the historic pilgrimage path.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near Villava/Atarrabia?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region boasts around 86 difficult routes. These often include significant climbs over mountain passes. Consider the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984948834/pamplona-irurita-return-via-belate-pass-and-eugiko-urtegia-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona - Irurita – return via Belate pass and Eugiko urtegia reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986655015/erro-pass-zubiri-loop-from-pamplona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erro Pass - Zubiri Loop from Pamplona&lt;/a&gt; for a notable climb over Erro Pass.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: The most pleasant times for road cycling in Villava/Atarrabia are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, temperatures are moderate, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be warm, especially in July and August, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potential for rain or snow in higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the Villava/Atarrabia area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984615802/aranguren-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranguren Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic countryside and returns to your starting point.

Q: What kind of attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by historical sites and offer scenic viewpoints. You might encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2707420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irulegi Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, look out for spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Ezkaba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2853366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Balsa de Ezkotitz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: The road cycling routes in Villava/Atarrabia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which includes river valleys, rolling hills, and challenging mountain passes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with good viewpoints in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: Absolutely. The region's varied terrain, including mountain passes and elevated sections, provides numerous opportunities for stunning views. Routes that ascend to higher points, such as those near Erro Pass or Belate Pass, often feature rewarding vistas of the surrounding valleys and mountains. Specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Monte Ezkaba&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: There are over 200 road cycling routes available in the Villava/Atarrabia region, offering a wide range of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling trails in Atarrabia?
A: Villava/Atarrabia is well-connected by local public transport, particularly bus services from Pamplona, which can help you reach various starting points for routes. However, specific routes may require a short ride from the nearest bus stop. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes for the most convenient access to your chosen trail.

Q: Are there less crowded road cycling trails in Villava/Atarrabia?
A: While popular routes like sections of the Camino Francés can see more activity, many of the region's 200+ routes, especially those venturing into the more mountainous or rural areas, offer a quieter cycling experience. Exploring routes during off-peak hours or weekdays can also help you find more solitude.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along road bike routes near Villava?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the Villava/Atarrabia area pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes, bakeries, and restaurants. These provide excellent opportunities to refuel and take a break. Planning your route with komoot allows you to see points of interest, including refreshment stops, along the way.
tips:
Zubiri is a town located in the Esteríbar valley that is surrounded by meadows and green mountains. In this town, of about 450 inhabitants, there are no more and no less than six hostels for pilgrims with prices ranging between nine and 16 euros per night (in bunk beds in shared rooms).
You will also find bakeries and a pharmacy, as well as several bars and restaurants.
You can't leave Zubiri without going to see the bridge of Rabia since Zubiri means "town of the bridge" in Basque.

faqs.question: Is the Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Yes, when utilizing the appropriate road cycling routes, this route is generally considered easy to moderate. It features gradual climbs and rolling sections, making it suitable for less experienced cyclists or those looking for a recovery ride. Be aware that the Camino de Santiago footpath sections are not suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows quiet secondary roads and sections of the N-135 highway, consisting largely of asphalt. While the Camino de Santiago footpath can be rugged, dedicated road options are available alongside these sections, ensuring a smooth ride for road bikes.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful and varied rural landscapes, passing through traditional Navarran villages, agricultural areas, and wooded stretches with beech and pine trees. A significant portion of the route runs alongside the Arga River, offering consistent scenic views, and you'll also catch glimpses of the Pyrenean foothills.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features will I see on the route?
faqs.answer: The route is a key section of the French Way of the Camino de Santiago. You'll encounter several medieval bridges along the Arga River, including the notable Puente de la Rabia in Zubiri and the Puente de la Magdalena near Pamplona. The route also passes through Villava, the birthplace of cyclist Miguel Induráin, and culminates in the historic city of Pamplona.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable cycling and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route follows public roads and paths, specific regulations for dogs can vary. Generally, dogs are allowed on public roads and trails in Spain, but they must be kept on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri route. It utilizes public roads and paths, making it freely accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: If starting in Zubiri, you can typically find parking within the town. For Pamplona, various public parking options are available in and around the city center. Public transport options, including buses, connect Pamplona and Zubiri, and some may accommodate bikes, though it's best to check with the local transport provider beforehand.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a significant section of the Camino Francés - 01, which is part of the Camino de Santiago. It also shares sections with the Tour of the Pamplona Basin, The Pathway of the Arga river, and Iruñerriko ibai-pasealekua, offering connections to other local and regional cycling and walking routes.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to eat along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects two towns and passes through several villages, you'll find various amenities. Both Zubiri and Pamplona offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops. Smaller villages along the way may also have options for refreshments, especially given its connection to the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What is the total distance and typical duration for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Clasica Pamplona - Zubiri route covers approximately 31.7 kilometers (19.7 miles). For a road cyclist, it typically takes around 1 hour and 27 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.
